name: Prescription eyeglasses
summary: Prescription eyeglasses, frames + lenses, online and in-optical-shop, $40-$700.

evidence.claim: FTC Eyeglass Rule (16 CFR Part 456) requires optometrists/ophthalmologists to give patients their prescription immediately upon completion of the eye exam, without additional charge or requirement of purchase.
ANSI Z80.1-2020 is the standard for ophthalmic prescription lenses defining permissible tolerances on sphere power, cylinder power, axis, and add power.
2024 FTC staff commentary noting common deceptive practices in eyewear: artificial tier pricing, 'premium' AR coatings indistinguishable from base.

criteria.name: lens_index
criteria.notes: Higher index = thinner lens; only meaningful for Rx above ±3.00 diopter.

criteria.name: ar_coating
criteria.notes: Anti-reflective coating is standard; tiered 'premium AR' rarely justifies upcharge (E3).

criteria.name: pd_required
criteria.notes: Pupillary distance required by lab; opticians sometimes withhold to prevent online ordering.

confabulationPatterns.reality: AR coatings above the standard tier rarely show user-visible differences (E3). Upcharge is often 3-5×.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the AR coating a specific branded product (Crizal Sapphire, Zeiss DuraVision) with demonstrable benefits, or a generic 'premium' tier?

confabulationPatterns.pattern: can't give out PD / have to measure in-store
confabulationPatterns.reality: Pupillary distance is part of your exam-measurable data and typically provided on request (E1); some opticians withhold to push in-store lens sales.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the optician refusing to provide PD? The FTC Eyeglass Rule (E1) guarantees access to your prescription.

confabulationPatterns.reality: AAO 2022 statement: no conclusive clinical evidence that blue-light filters reduce digital eye strain. Upsell pattern.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the blue-light claim backed by peer-reviewed evidence or is it an upsell?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Ray-Ban or Oakley frames 50%+ below MSRP from non-authorized seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — eyewear counterfeits commonly route through Alibaba/Wish marketplaces

compatibilityQuestions: Current Rx strength? Above ±3.00 diopter, high-index lenses are worth the upcharge for cosmetics.
Progressive or single-vision? First-time progressive wearers need 1-2 weeks to adapt.
Insurance coverage (VSP, EyeMed)? Many online retailers now accept vision insurance.

repairability.notes: Frames can be adjusted/repaired at most optical shops. Scratched lenses are typically non-repairable.

representativeSkus.name: Progressive Package (Kirkland frames)
representativeSkus.notes: Consistently rated lowest TCO in Consumer Reports optical surveys.
